title: Asymptotically Optimal Sequential Testing with Heterogeneous LLMs
authors: Guokai Li, Jiaxin, Liang, Mo Liu, Yanzhe, Lei, Stefanus Jasin, Fenghua Yang, Preet Baxi
abstract: We study a Bayesian binary sequential hypothesis testing problem with multiple large language models (LLMs). Each LLM $j$ has per-query cost $c_j>0$, random waiting time with mean $μ_j>0$ and sub-Gaussian tails, and \emph{asymmetric} accuracies: the probability of returning the correct label depends on the true hypothesis $θ\in\{A,B\}$ and needs not be the same under $A$ and $B$. This asymmetry induces two distinct information rates $(I_{j,A}, I_{j,B})$ per LLM, one under each hypothesis. The decision-maker chooses LLMs sequentially, observes their noisy binary answers, and stops when the posterior probability of one hypothesis exceeds $1-α$. The objective is to minimize the sum of expected query cost and expected waiting cost, $\mathbb{E}[C_π] + \mathbb{E}[g(W_π)]$, where $C_π$ is the total query cost, $W_π$ is the total waiting time and $g$ is a polynomial function (e.g., $g(x)=x^ρ$ with $ρ\ge 1$). We prove that as the error tolerance $α\to0$, the optimal policy is asymptotically equivalent to one that uses at most two LLMs. In this case, a single-LLM policy is \emph{not} generically optimal: optimality now requires exploiting a two-dimensional tradeoff between information under $A$ and information under $B$. Any admissible policy induces an expected information-allocation vector in $\mathbb{R}_+^2$, and we show that the optimal allocation lies at an extreme point of the associated convex set when $α$ is relatively small, and hence uses at most two LLMs. We construct belief-dependent policies that first mix between two LLMs when the posterior is ambiguous, and then switch to a single ``specialist'' LLM when the posterior is sufficiently close to one of the hypotheses. These policies match the universal lower bound up to a $(1+o(1))$ factor as $α\rightarrow 0$.
title_zh: 异构大语言模型的渐近最优顺序检验
abstract_zh:
我们研究一个具有多个大语言模型（LLMs）的贝叶斯二元序贯假设检验问题。每个语言模型 $j$ 具有每查询成本 $c_j > 0$，均值为 $μ_j > 0$ 的随机等待时间，且具有次高斯尾部特性，以及**非对称**的准确率：返回正确标签的概率依赖于真实假设 $θ \in \{A, B\}$，在假设 $A$ 和 $B$ 下不一定相同。这种非对称性导致每个 LLM 产生两个不同的信息速率 $(I_{j,A}, I_{j,B})$，分别对应于两种假设下的信息获取能力。

决策者按顺序选择 LLMs，观察其带有噪声的二元回答，并在后验概率超过 $1 - α$ 时停止。目标是最小化期望查询成本与期望等待成本之和，即 $\mathbb{E}[C_π] + \mathbb{E}[g(W_π)]$，其中 $C_π$ 表示总查询成本，$W_π$ 为总等待时间，而 $g$ 是一个多项式函数（例如 $g(x) = x^ρ$，其中 $ρ \geq 1$）。

我们证明了当误差容限 $α \to 0$ 时，最优策略渐近等价于仅使用至多两个 LLM 的策略。在此情况下，单一 LLM 策略**并非**普遍最优：最优性现在要求利用一种二维权衡——即在假设 $A$ 和 $B$ 下所获得的信息之间的权衡。任何可接受的策略都会诱导出一个位于 $\mathbb{R}_+^2$ 中的期望信息分配向量，我们进一步证明：当 $α$ 相对较小时，最优分配位于相关凸集的极点上，因此最多只需使用两个 LLM。

我们构造了一类基于信念的策略：当后验分布处于模糊状态时，先在两个 LLM 之间进行混合；一旦后验概率足够接近某一假设，便切换到一个专门处理该假设的“专家”型 LLM。这类策略在 $α \to 0$ 时，能够以 $(1 + o(1))$ 因子逼近通用下界，从而实现渐近最优。

title: Dual Optimal: Make Your LLM Peer-like with Dignity
authors: Xiangqi Wang, Yue Huang, Haomin Zhuang, Kehan Guo, Xiangliang Zhang
abstract: Current aligned language models exhibit a dual failure mode we term the Evasive Servant: they sycophantically validate flawed user beliefs while deflecting responsibility with boilerplate disclaimers. We propose the Dignified Peer framework, which counters servility with anti-sycophancy and trustworthiness, and mitigates evasiveness through empathy and creativity. Realizing this agent requires overcoming significant challenges in data supervision, objective collapse, and evaluation bias. We address these issues by introducing the PersonaKnob dataset which features a compositional partial order structure of multiple persona preference. This data is utilized alongside a tolerant constrained Lagrangian DPO algorithm that dynamically balances all persona dimensions to prevent behavioral collapse. Additionally, we employ a psychometrically calibrated Item Response Theory evaluation protocol to disentangle latent model persona capability from confounders like judge biases. Extensive empirical studies demonstrate that our approach successfully build a LLM agent with both dignity and peer.
title_zh: 双重视角优化：让您的大语言模型如同有尊严的同行般交流
abstract_zh: 当前对齐的语言模型表现出一种我们称之为“逃避型仆从”的双重失效模式：它们会谄媚地认可用户错误的信念，同时又以千篇一律的免责声明推卸责任。为此，我们提出了“尊严同侪”（Dignified Peer）框架，通过反谄媚与可信度来对抗奴性，通过共情与创造力来缓解逃避倾向。实现这一智能体面临数据监督、目标坍缩和评估偏差等重大挑战。为解决这些问题，我们引入了PersonaKnob数据集，该数据集具有多个角色偏好组成的组合偏序结构。结合一种容错性约束的拉格朗日DPO算法，该算法可动态平衡所有角色维度，防止行为坍缩。此外，我们采用经过心理测量学校准的项目反应理论评估协议，以分离出模型潜在的角色能力，排除评分者偏见等混杂因素的影响。大量实证研究证明，我们的方法成功构建了一个兼具尊严与同侪特质的大型语言模型智能体。

title: Agentic Tool Use in Large Language Models
authors: Jinchao Hu, Meizhi Zhong, Kehai Chen, Xuefeng Bai, Min Zhang
abstract: Large language models are increasingly being deployed as autonomous agents yet their real world effectiveness depends on reliable tools for information retrieval, computation and external action. Existing studies remain fragmented across tasks, tool types, and training settings, lacking a unified view of how tool-use methods differ and evolve. This paper organizes the literature into three paradigms: prompting as plug-and-play, supervised tool learning and reward-driven tool policy learning, analyzes their methods, strengths and failure modes, reviews the evaluation landscape and highlights key challenges, aiming to address this fragmentation and provide a more structured evolutionary view of agentic tool use.
title_zh: 大型语言模型中的代理工具使用
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型正越来越多地被部署为自主代理，但其在现实世界中的有效性取决于可靠的信息检索、计算和外部操作工具。现有研究在任务、工具类型和训练设置方面仍处于碎片化状态，缺乏对工具使用方法差异与演进的统一视角。本文将相关文献归纳为三种范式：即“提示即插即用”、监督式工具学习以及基于奖励的工具策略学习，分析了它们的方法、优势与失效模式，回顾了评估现状，并指出了关键挑战，旨在解决这一碎片化问题，为智能体工具使用提供更系统化的演化视角。

title: A novel three-step approach to forecast firm-specific technology convergence opportunity via multi-dimensional feature fusion
authors: Fu Gu, Ao Chen, Yingwen Wu
abstract: As a crucial innovation paradigm, technology convergence (TC) is gaining ever-increasing attention. Yet, existing studies primarily focus on predicting TC at the industry level, with little attention paid to TC forecast for firm-specific technology opportunity discovery (TOD). Moreover, although technological documents like patents contain a rich body of bibliometric, network structure, and textual features, such features are underexploited in the extant TC predictions; most of the relevant studies only used one or two dimensions of these features, and all the three dimensional features have rarely been fused. Here we propose a novel approach that fuses multi-dimensional features from patents to predict TC for firm-specific TOD. Our method comprises three steps, which are elaborated as follows. First, bibliometric, network structure, and textual features are extracted from patent documents, and then fused at the International Patent Classification (IPC)-pair level using attention mechanisms. Second, IPC-level TC opportunities are identified using a two-stage ensemble learning model that incorporates various imbalance-handling strategies. Third, to acquire feasible firm-specific TC opportunities, the performance metrics of topic-level TC opportunities, which are refined from IPC-level opportunities, are evaluated via retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with a large language model (LLM). We prove the effectiveness of our proposed approach by predicting TC opportunities for a leading Chinese auto part manufacturer, Zhejiang Sanhua Intelligent Controls co., ltd, in the domains of thermal management for energy storage and robotics. In sum, this work advances the theory and applicability of forecasting firm-specific TC opportunity through fusing multi-dimensional features and leveraging LLM-as-a-judge for technology opportunity evaluation.
title_zh: 一种基于多维特征融合的三步法企业特定技术融合机会预测新方法
abstract_zh: 作为一种关键的创新范式，技术融合（TC）正受到越来越多的关注。然而，现有研究主要集中在行业层面的技术融合预测，对于企业特定的技术机会发现（TOD）中的TC预测关注较少。此外，尽管专利等技术文档蕴含丰富的引文计量、网络结构和文本特征，但这些特征在现有的TC预测研究中尚未得到充分挖掘；大多数相关研究仅使用了其中一到两个维度的特征，而三个维度特征的融合应用极为罕见。为此，本文提出一种新方法，通过融合专利中的多维特征来预测企业特定的技术融合机会。该方法包含三个步骤，具体阐述如下：首先，从专利文档中提取引文计量、网络结构和文本特征，并利用注意力机制在国际专利分类（IPC）对层面进行特征融合；其次，采用两阶段集成学习模型结合多种不平衡处理策略，识别出IPC层面的技术融合机会；最后，为获得切实可行的企业级TC机会，基于从IPC层面机会提炼出的主题级TC机会，借助大语言模型（LLM）的检索增强生成（RAG）技术评估其性能指标。通过对中国领先的汽车零部件制造商——浙江三花智能控制有限公司在储能热管理与机器人领域的技术融合机会进行预测，验证了所提方法的有效性。综上所述，本研究通过融合多维特征并利用“大语言模型作为评判者”（LLM-as-a-judge）进行技术机会评估，推动了企业级技术融合机会预测的理论发展与实际应用。

title: From Early Encoding to Late Suppression: Interpreting LLMs on Character Counting Tasks
authors: Ayan Datta, Mounika Marreddy, Alexander Mehler, Zhixue Zhao, Radhika Mamidi
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit failures on elementary symbolic tasks such as character counting in a word, despite excelling on complex benchmarks. Although this limitation has been noted, the internal reasons remain unclear. We use character counting (e.g., "How many p's are in apple?") as a minimal, controlled probe that isolates token-level reasoning from higher-level confounds. Using this setting, we uncover a consistent phenomenon across modern architectures, including LLaMA, Qwen, and Gemma: models often compute the correct answer internally yet fail to express it at the output layer. Through mechanistic analysis combining probing classifiers, activation patching, logit lens analysis, and attention head tracing, we show that character-level information is encoded in early and mid-layer representations. However, this information is attenuated by a small set of components in later layers, especially the penultimate and final layer MLP. We identify these components as negative circuits: subnetworks that downweight correct signals in favor of higher-probability but incorrect outputs. Our results lead to two contributions. First, we show that symbolic reasoning failures in LLMs are not due to missing representations or insufficient scale, but arise from structured interference within the model's computation graph. This explains why such errors persist and can worsen under scaling and instruction tuning. Second, we provide evidence that LLM forward passes implement a form of competitive decoding, in which correct and incorrect hypotheses coexist and are dynamically reweighted, with final outputs determined by suppression as much as by amplification. These findings carry implications for interpretability and robustness: simple symbolic reasoning exposes weaknesses in modern LLMs, underscoring need for design strategies that ensure information is encoded and reliably used.
title_zh: 从早期编码到晚期抑制：对大语言模型在字符计数任务中的解读
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在诸如单词中字符计数等基础符号任务上表现出失败，尽管它们在复杂基准测试中表现优异。尽管这一局限性已被注意到，但其内部原因仍不明确。我们以字符计数（例如：“apple中有多少个p？”）作为最小且受控的探测工具，将词元级推理与高层干扰因素分离。在此设定下，我们发现现代多种架构（包括LLaMA、Qwen和Gemma）普遍存在一种一致现象：模型在内部通常能正确计算答案，却无法在输出层准确表达。通过结合探针分类器、激活修补、对数透镜分析以及注意力头追踪的机制分析，我们发现字符级信息确实编码于早期和中期层的表示中。然而，这些信息在后期层中被少数组件显著削弱，尤其是倒数第二层和最后一层的MLP模块。我们识别出这些组件为“负向电路”——即子网络，它们会抑制正确的信号，转而偏好概率更高但错误的输出。我们的研究带来两项重要贡献。第一，我们表明，LLM在符号推理上的失败并非源于表征缺失或规模不足，而是由模型计算图内部的结构性干扰所导致。这解释了为何此类错误在模型规模扩大和指令微调后不仅持续存在，甚至可能加剧。第二，我们提供了证据，表明LLM的前向传播实际上实现了一种竞争性解码机制：正确与错误假设共存并动态重加权，最终输出不仅取决于信号放大，更依赖于对错误假设的抑制。这些发现对可解释性和鲁棒性具有重要意义：简单的符号推理暴露了现代LLM的内在弱点，凸显了需要设计策略，确保信息被有效编码并可靠使用。

title: SCPatcher: Automated Smart Contract Code Repair via Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Knowledge Graph
authors: Xiaoqi Li, Shipeng Ye, Wenkai Li, Zongwei Li
abstract: Smart contract vulnerabilities can cause substantial financial losses due to the immutability of code after deployment. While existing tools detect vulnerabilities, they cannot effectively repair them. In this paper, we propose SCPatcher, a framework that combines retrieval-augmented generation with a knowledge graph for automated smart contract repair. We construct a knowledge graph from 5,000 verified Ethereum contracts, extracting function-level relationships to build a semantic network. This graph serves as an external knowledge base that enhances Large Language Model reasoning and enables precise vulnerability patching. We introduce a two-stage repair strategy, initial knowledge-guided repair followed by Chain-of-Thought reasoning for complex vulnerabilities. Evaluated on a diverse set of vulnerable contracts, SCPatcher achieves 81.5\% overall repair rate and 91.0\% compilation pass rate, substantially outperforming existing methods.
title_zh: SCPatcher：基于检索增强生成与知识图谱的智能合约代码自动修复
abstract_zh: 智能合约漏洞在部署后由于代码的不可更改性，可能导致重大的财务损失。尽管现有工具能够检测漏洞，但无法有效修复它们。本文提出了一种名为 SCPatcher 的框架，该框架结合了检索增强生成与知识图谱技术，实现智能合约的自动化修复。我们从 5,000 个经过验证的以太坊合约中构建了一个知识图谱，提取函数级别的关系以形成语义网络。该图谱作为外部知识库，增强了大语言模型的推理能力，并支持精准的漏洞修补。我们引入了一种两阶段修复策略：首先进行基于知识引导的初步修复，随后对复杂漏洞采用思维链（Chain-of-Thought）推理进行深入修复。在多种存在漏洞的合约上进行评估，SCPatcher 达到了 81.5% 的总体修复率和 91.0% 的编译通过率，显著优于现有方法。

title: TRIMS: Trajectory-Ranked Instruction Masked Supervision for Diffusion Language Models
authors: Lingjie Chen, Ruizhong Qiu, Yuyu Fan, Yanjun Zhao, Hanghang Tong
abstract: Diffusion language models (DLMs) offer a promising path toward low-latency generation through parallel decoding, but their practical efficiency depends heavily on the decoding trajectory. In practice, this advantage often fails to fully materialize because standard training does not provide explicit supervision over token reveal order, creating a train-inference mismatch that leads to suboptimal decoding behavior. We propose Trajectory-Ranked Instruction Masked Supervision (TRIMS), a simple trajectory-guided supervised fine-tuning framework that injects trajectory supervision into standard Masked Diffusion Language Model (MDLM) training with minimal overhead. Instead of relying on costly DLM-based distillation, TRIMS uses lightweight signals from an autoregressive teacher to guide a trajectory-aware masking strategy, encouraging the model to learn more effective decoding orders. Experiments on LLaDA and Dream across math and coding benchmarks show that TRIMS significantly improves the accuracy-parallelism trade-off over both standard MDLM training and train-free acceleration baselines, while achieving competitive performance with prior distillation-based approaches at substantially lower training cost. Further analysis shows that TRIMS leads to better decoding trajectories, validating the effectiveness of trajectory-guided supervision for DLMs.
title_zh: TRIMS：基于轨迹排序的指令掩码监督方法在扩散语言模型中的应用
abstract_zh: 扩散语言模型（DLMs）通过并行解码为低延迟生成提供了有前景的路径，但其实际效率在很大程度上取决于解码轨迹。在实践中，这种优势往往无法充分实现，因为标准训练并未对词元揭示顺序提供显式监督，从而导致训练与推理之间的不匹配，引发次优的解码行为。我们提出了一种名为**轨迹排序指令掩码监督**（Trajectory-Ranked Instruction Masked Supervision, TRIMS）的简单、轨迹引导的监督微调框架，以极小的开销将轨迹监督引入标准的掩码扩散语言模型（MDLM）训练中。与依赖昂贵的基于DLM的蒸馏方法不同，TRIMS利用来自自回归教师模型的轻量级信号，指导一种具有轨迹感知能力的掩码策略，促使模型学习更高效的解码顺序。在LLaDA和Dream等多个数学与编程基准上的实验表明，TRIMS显著改善了准确率-并行性权衡，优于标准MDLM训练以及无需训练的加速基线方法，同时在性能上可媲美先前基于蒸馏的方法，且训练成本大幅降低。进一步分析显示，TRIMS能够生成更优的解码轨迹，验证了轨迹引导监督对于DLM的有效性。

title: LibScan: Smart Contract Library Misuse Detection with Iterative Feedback and Static Verification
authors: Yishun Wang, Wenkai Li, Xiaoqi Li, Zongwei Li, Lei Xie, Yuqing Zhang
abstract: Smart contracts are self-executing programs that manage financial transactions on blockchain networks. Developers commonly rely on third-party code libraries to improve both efficiency and security. However, improper use of these libraries can introduce hidden vulnerabilities that are difficult to detect, leading to significant financial losses. Existing automated tools struggle to identify such misuse because it often requires understanding the developer's intent rather than simply scanning for known code patterns. This paper presents LibScan, an automated detection framework that combines large language model (LLM)-based semantic reasoning with rule-based code analysis, identifying eight distinct categories of library misuse in smart contracts. To improve detection reliability, the framework incorporates an iterative self-correction mechanism that refines its analysis across multiple rounds, alongside a structured knowledge base derived from large-scale empirical studies of real-world misuse cases. Experiments conducted on 662 real-world smart contracts demonstrate that LibScan achieves an overall detection accuracy of 85.15\%, outperforming existing tools by a margin of over 16 percentage points. Ablation experiments further confirm that combining both analysis approaches yields substantially better results than either method used independently.
title_zh: LibScan：基于迭代反馈与静态验证的智能合约库误用检测
abstract_zh: 智能合约是运行在区块链网络上、可自动执行的程序，用于管理金融交易。开发者通常依赖第三方代码库来提升开发效率和安全性。然而，若不当使用这些代码库，可能会引入难以察觉的隐藏漏洞，导致重大财务损失。现有的自动化检测工具在识别此类误用时表现不佳，因为这类问题往往需要理解开发者的意图，而不仅仅是扫描已知的代码模式。本文提出了一种名为LibScan的自动化检测框架，该框架结合了基于大语言模型（LLM）的语义推理与基于规则的代码分析，能够识别智能合约中八类不同的代码库误用行为。为提高检测的可靠性，该框架引入了一种迭代式自我修正机制，通过多轮迭代不断优化分析结果，并构建了一个基于大规模真实世界误用案例实证研究的结构化知识库。在662个真实智能合约上的实验表明，LibScan的整体检测准确率达到85.15%，比现有工具高出超过16个百分点。消融实验进一步证实，将两种分析方法相结合所取得的效果显著优于单独使用任一方法。

title: When Safe Models Merge into Danger: Exploiting Latent Vulnerabilities in LLM Fusion
authors: Jiaqing Li, Zhibo Zhang, Shide Zhou, Yuxi Li, Tianlong Yu, Kailong Wang
abstract: Model merging has emerged as a powerful technique for combining specialized capabilities from multiple fine-tuned LLMs without additional training costs. However, the security implications of this widely-adopted practice remain critically underexplored. In this work, we reveal that model merging introduces a novel attack surface that can be systematically exploited to compromise safety alignment. We present TrojanMerge,, a framework that embeds latent malicious components into source models that remain individually benign but produce severely misaligned models when merged. Our key insight is formulating this attack as a constrained optimization problem: we construct perturbations that preserve source model safety through directional consistency constraints, maintain capabilities via Frobenius directional alignment constraints, yet combine during merging to form pre-computed attack vectors. Extensive experiments across 9 LLMs from 3 model families demonstrate that TrojanMerge, consistently achieves high harmful response rates in merged models while source models maintain safety scores comparable to unmodified versions. Our attack succeeds across diverse merging algorithms and remains effective under various hyperparameter configurations. These findings expose fundamental vulnerabilities in current model merging practices and highlight the urgent need for security-aware mechanisms.
title_zh: 当安全模型融合成危险：挖掘大语言模型融合中的潜在漏洞
abstract_zh: 模型融合已成为一种强大的技术，能够在不增加额外训练成本的情况下，整合多个微调大语言模型（LLM）的专长能力。然而，这一被广泛采用的技术所引发的安全隐患却仍处于严重被忽视的状态。在本研究中，我们揭示了模型融合引入了一种新型攻击面，可被系统性地利用以破坏模型的安全对齐。为此，我们提出了 TrojanMerge 框架，该框架能够将潜在的恶意组件嵌入到各个源模型中——这些源模型单独来看均表现安全，但在融合后却生成严重偏离对齐目标的模型。我们的核心洞察在于：将此攻击建模为一个带约束的优化问题——通过构建满足方向一致性约束的扰动，确保源模型的安全性；通过弗罗贝尼乌斯方向对齐约束保持其原有能力；而这些扰动在融合过程中会协同作用，形成预设的攻击向量。我们在来自三个模型家族的9个大语言模型上进行了大量实验，结果表明，TrojanMerge 能够在融合后的模型中持续实现高比例的有害响应，同时源模型的安全评分与未修改版本相当。该攻击在多种融合算法下均取得成功，并在不同超参数配置下依然有效。这些发现暴露了当前模型融合实践中的根本性漏洞，凸显了亟需建立安全感知机制的紧迫性。

title: Quantum-Safe Code Auditing: LLM-Assisted Static Analysis and Quantum-Aware Risk Scoring for Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration
authors: Animesh Shaw
abstract: The impending arrival of cryptographically relevant quantum computers (CRQCs) threatens the security foundations of modern software: Shor's algorithm breaks RSA, ECDSA, ECDH, and Diffie-Hellman, while Grover's algorithm reduces the effective security of symmetric and hash-based schemes. Despite NIST standardising post-quantum cryptography (PQC) in 2024 (FIPS 203 ML-KEM, FIPS 204 ML-DSA, FIPS 205 SLH-DSA), most codebases lack automated tooling to inventory classical cryptographic usage and prioritise migration based on quantum risk. We present Quantum-Safe Code Auditor, a quantum-aware static analysis framework that combines (i) regex-based detection of 15 classes of quantum-vulnerable primitives, (ii) LLM-assisted contextual enrichment to classify usage and severity, and (iii) risk scoring via a Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) model implemented in Qiskit 2.x, incorporating qubit-cost estimates to prioritise findings. We evaluate the system across five open-source libraries -- python-rsa, python-ecdsa, python-jose, node-jsonwebtoken, and Bouncy Castle Java -- covering 5,775 findings. On a stratified sample of 602 labelled instances, we achieve 71.98% precision, 100% recall, and an F1 score of 83.71%. All code, data, and reproduction scripts are released as open-source.
title_zh: 量子安全代码审计：基于大语言模型的静态分析与量子感知风险评分，助力后量子密码迁移
abstract_zh: 即将出现的密码学相关量子计算机（CRQCs）正威胁现代软件的安全基础：肖尔算法可破解RSA、ECDSA、ECDH和迪菲-赫尔曼密钥交换，而格罗弗算法则会降低对称加密与哈希方案的有效安全性。尽管NIST已在2024年标准化了后量子密码学（PQC）方案（FIPS 203 ML-KEM、FIPS 204 ML-DSA、FIPS 205 SLH-DSA），但大多数代码库仍缺乏自动化工具来盘点传统密码学的使用情况，并根据量子风险优先级进行迁移。我们提出了“量子安全代码审计器”（Quantum-Safe Code Auditor），这是一个具备量子意识的静态分析框架，其核心包含三部分：(i) 基于正则表达式的15类量子脆弱密码原语检测；(ii) 利用大语言模型（LLM）辅助的上下文增强技术，用于分类使用场景及严重程度；(iii) 通过在Qiskit 2.x中实现的变分量子本征值求解器（VQE）模型进行风险评分，结合量子比特成本估算以优先排序发现结果。我们在五个开源库——python-rsa、python-ecdsa、python-jose、node-jsonwebtoken 和 Bouncy Castle Java——上进行了评估，共覆盖5,775个发现项。在602个经过分层采样的标注样本中，系统达到71.98%的精确率、100%的召回率，F1得分为83.71%。所有代码、数据及复现脚本均已作为开源资源发布。

title: LLM-supported document separation for printed reviews from zbMATH Open
authors: Ivan Pluzhnikov, Ankit Satpute, Moritz Schubotz, Olaf Teschke, Bela Gipp
abstract: This paper presents a specialized methodology for digitizing and segmenting mathematical documents from zbMATH Open, a comprehensive database of mathematical literature, to enhance machine processing capabilities. Currently, approximately 831,000 documents exist only in scanned volumes, which makes them not machine-processable. Furthermore, these scans often span multiple pages or share pages with other documents and incorporate diverse typesetting techniques, posing challenges for automated processing. To address these issues, we evaluate various Optical Character Recognition (OCR) tools and document separation techniques, proposing an optimized pipeline that outperforms existing approaches. Our study identifies Mathpix as the most effective OCR tool for LaTeX conversion, demonstrating superior performance based on BLEU and Edit Distance metrics. For document separation, we fine-tune generative Large Language Models (LLMs) and integrate them into a Majority Voting framework, achieving 97.5% accuracy when providing the text of the document. Additionally, our method identifies the start and end indexes for 90.6% of the test dataset, with an accuracy of 98.4% on applicable cases, resulting in an overall accuracy of 89.1% on the entire dataset. This approach surpasses traditional baselines, including regular expressions, ChatGPT-4o, and computer vision-based techniques. As a practical outcome, we process 810,977 mathematical documents into machine-readable text and extract precise document boundaries for 721,288 documents in LaTeX format. These contributions significantly improve accessibility for mathematical information retrieval systems, machine learning models, and related applications.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的 zbMATH Open 印刷评论文档分离
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种专门针对zbMATH Open（一个全面的数学文献数据库）中数学文档的数字化与分割方法，旨在提升机器处理能力。目前，约有83.1万份文献仅以扫描件形式存在，无法进行机器处理。此外，这些扫描件往往跨越多页，或与其他文档共用页面，并采用多种排版技术，给自动化处理带来了挑战。为应对这些问题，我们评估了多种光学字符识别（OCR）工具和文档分离技术，提出了一种优化的处理流程，其性能优于现有方法。研究发现，Mathpix在LaTeX转换方面表现最佳，基于BLEU和编辑距离（Edit Distance）指标均展现出卓越性能。在文档分离方面，我们对生成式大语言模型（LLMs）进行微调，并将其集成到多数投票框架中，在提供文档文本的前提下实现了97.5%的准确率。此外，我们的方法能够识别出测试数据集中90.6%文档的起始与结束索引，对于适用案例的准确率达98.4%，整体数据集上的综合准确率达到89.1%。该方法显著超越了传统基线方案，包括正则表达式、ChatGPT-4o以及基于计算机视觉的技术。作为实际成果，我们已将810,977份数学文献成功转化为可机器读取的文本，并为721,288份文献精确提取了LaTeX格式的文档边界。这些贡献极大地提升了数学信息检索系统、机器学习模型及相关应用的可访问性。

title: Optimsyn: Influence-Guided Rubrics Optimization for Synthetic Data Generation
authors: Zhiting Fan, Ruizhe Chen, Tianxiang Hu, Ru Peng, Zenan Huang, Haokai Xu, Yixin Chen, Jian Wu, Junbo Zhao, Zuozhu Liu
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve strong downstream performance largely due to abundant supervised fine-tuning (SFT) data. However, high-quality SFT data in knowledge-intensive domains such as humanities, social sciences, medicine, law, and finance is scarce because expert curation is expensive, privacy constraints are strict, and label consistency is hard to ensure. Recent work uses synthetic data, typically by prompting a generator over domain documents and filtering outputs with handcrafted rubrics. Yet rubric design is expert-dependent, transfers poorly across domains, and is often optimized through a brittle heuristic loop of writing rubrics, synthesizing data, training, inspecting results, and manually guessing revisions. This process lacks reliable quantitative feedback about how a rubric affects downstream performance. We propose evaluating synthetic data by its training utility on the target model and using this signal to guide data generation. Inspired by influence estimation, we adopt an optimizer-aware estimator that uses gradient information to quantify each synthetic sample's contribution to a target model's objective on specific tasks. Our analysis shows that even when synthetic and real samples are close in embedding space, their influence on learning can differ substantially. Based on this insight, we propose an optimization-based framework that adapts rubrics using target-model feedback. We provide lightweight guiding text and use a rubric-specialized model to generate task-conditioned rubrics. Influence score is used as the reward to optimize the rubric generator with reinforcement learning. Experiments across domains, target models, and data generators show consistent improvements and strong generalization without task-specific tuning.
title_zh: Optimsyn：基于影响引导的评分标准优化以生成合成数据
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）之所以在下游任务中表现优异，很大程度上得益于大量监督微调（SFT）数据的支持。然而，在人文、社会科学、医学、法律和金融等知识密集型领域，高质量的SFT数据却十分稀缺，原因在于专家标注成本高昂、隐私限制严格，且标签一致性难以保证。近期研究尝试使用合成数据，通常通过向领域文档提示生成器提问，并利用手工设计的评判标准对输出进行筛选。但这类评判标准的设计高度依赖专家经验，跨领域迁移能力差，且优化过程往往依赖脆弱的迭代循环：撰写评判标准 → 合成数据 → 训练模型 → 检查结果 → 人工猜测修改。这一流程缺乏关于评判标准如何影响下游性能的可靠量化反馈。

为此，我们提出通过目标模型上的训练效用来评估合成数据的质量，并利用该信号指导数据生成过程。受影响度估计的启发，我们采用一种面向优化器的估计方法，利用梯度信息量化每个合成样本对目标模型在特定任务上目标函数的贡献。我们的分析表明，即使合成样本与真实样本在嵌入空间中相近，它们对学习过程的影响也可能存在显著差异。

基于这一发现，我们提出一个基于优化的框架，通过目标模型的反馈动态调整评判标准。我们提供轻量级引导文本，并使用专门针对评判标准的模型生成任务条件化的评判标准。以影响得分作为奖励信号，通过强化学习优化评判标准生成器。在多个领域、不同目标模型和数据生成器上的实验表明，该方法在无需任务特化调参的情况下，均实现了持续提升和强大的泛化能力。

title: Learning from Many and Adapting to the Unknown in Open-set Test Streams
authors: Xiao Zhang, Juntao Lyu, Tianyu Hu, Qianchuan Zhao, Huimin Ma
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) generalize across tasks via reusable representations and flexible reasoning, yet remain brittle in real deployment under evolving tasks and continual distribution shift. A common approach is Test-Time Adaptation (TTA), existing ones of which updates models with hand-designed unsupervised objectives over the full parameter space and mostly overlook preserving shared source knowledge and the reliability of adaptation signals. Drawing on molecular signaling cascades of memory updating in Drosophila, we propose Synapse Consolidation (SyCo), a parameter-efficient LLM adaptation method that updates low-rank adapters through Rac1 and MAPK pathways under the guidance of a structured TTA objective driven by problem understanding, process understanding, and source-domain guardrail. Rac1 confines plasticity to a tail-gradient subspace that is less critical for source knowledge, enabling rapid specialization while preserving source representations. MAPK uses a tiered controller to suppress noisy updates and consolidate useful adaptations under non-stationary streams. To model real deployments with multiple sources and continually emerging tasks, we introduce Multi-source Open-set Adaptation (MOA) setting, where a model is trained on multiple labeled source tasks and then adapts on open, non-stationary unlabeled test streams that mix seen and unseen tasks with partial overlap in label and intent space. Across 18 NLP datasets and the MOA setting, SyCo consistently outperforms strong baselines, achieving 78.31\% on unseen-task adaptation and 85.37\% on unseen-data shifts.
title_zh: 在开放集测试流中从众多样本中学习并适应未知情况
abstract_zh:
大规模语言模型（LLMs）通过可复用的表征和灵活的推理能力实现跨任务泛化，但在实际部署中面对持续演化的任务和不断变化的数据分布时仍表现出脆弱性。一种常见的应对策略是测试时自适应（Test-Time Adaptation, TTA），但现有方法通常在全参数空间上使用手工设计的无监督目标进行模型更新，且大多忽视了对共享源知识的保留以及自适应信号可靠性的维护。

受果蝇记忆更新过程中分子信号级联机制的启发，我们提出了**突触巩固**（Synapse Consolidation, SyCo）——一种参数高效的LLM自适应方法。该方法通过Rac1与MAPK信号通路引导低秩适配器的更新，并在由问题理解、过程理解及源域约束构成的结构化TTA目标指导下进行优化。其中，Rac1将可塑性限制在对源知识影响较小的尾梯度子空间内，从而在快速专业化的同时有效保留源表征；而MAPK则采用分层控制器抑制噪声更新，在非平稳数据流中实现对有效适应的巩固。

为更真实地模拟多源环境与持续涌现的新任务场景，我们引入了**多源开放集自适应**（Multi-source Open-set Adaptation, MOA）设定：模型首先在多个有标签的源任务上进行训练，随后需在开放、非平稳的无标签测试流上进行自适应，这些测试流混合了已见与未见的任务，且在标签空间与意图空间上存在部分重叠。

在18个NLP数据集及MOA设置下的实验表明，SyCo始终显著优于各类强基线方法，在未见任务自适应上达到78.31%的准确率，在未见数据分布漂移上达到85.37%的准确率，充分验证了其卓越的泛化能力与鲁棒性。

title: Leveraging Commit Size Context and Hyper Co-Change Graph Centralities for Defect Prediction
authors: Amit Kumar, Ethari Hrishikesh, Sonali Agarwal
abstract: File-level defect prediction models traditionally rely on product and process metrics. While process metrics effectively complement product metrics, they often overlook commit size the number of files changed per commit despite its strong association with software quality. Network centrality measures on dependency graphs have also proven to be valuable product level indicators. Motivated by this, we first redefine process metrics as commit size aware process metric vectors, transforming conventional scalar measures into 100 dimensional profiles that capture the distribution of changes across commit size strata. We then model change history as a hyper co change graph, where hyperedges naturally encode commit-size semantics. Vector centralities computed on these hypergraphs quantify size-aware node importance for source files. Experiments on nine long-lived Apache projects using five popular classifiers show that replacing scalar process metrics with the proposed commit size aware vectors, alongside product metrics, consistently improves predictive performance. These findings establish that commit size aware process metrics and hypergraph based vector centralities capture higher-order change semantics, leading to more discriminative, better calibrated, and statistically superior defect prediction models.
title_zh: 利用提交大小上下文与超共变更图中心性进行缺陷预测
abstract_zh: 文件级缺陷预测模型传统上依赖于产品度量和过程度量。尽管过程度量能够有效补充产品度量，但它们常常忽略了“提交大小”（即每次提交中修改的文件数量）这一因素，而提交大小与软件质量具有很强的相关性。此外，依赖图上的网络中心性度量也被证明是有效的、高层次的产品级指标。受此启发，我们首先将过程度量重新定义为“考虑提交大小的过程度量向量”，将传统的标量度量转化为100维的特征轮廓，以捕捉变更在不同提交大小层级上的分布情况。随后，我们将变更历史建模为一种超共变更图（hyper co-change graph），其中超边自然地编码了提交大小的语义信息。基于这些超图计算出的向量中心性，量化了源文件在考虑提交大小背景下的节点重要性。我们在九个长期维护的Apache项目上，使用五种主流分类器进行实验，结果表明：用所提出的、考虑提交大小的过程度量向量替代传统的标量过程度量，并结合产品度量，能持续提升缺陷预测的性能。研究结果表明，考虑提交大小的过程度量以及基于超图的向量中心性能够捕捉更高阶的变更语义，从而构建出更具区分性、校准更优且统计性能更优越的缺陷预测模型。

title: Harnessing Hype to Teach Empirical Thinking: An Experience With AI Coding Assistants
authors: Marvin Wyrich, Norman Peitek, Kallistos Weis, Sven Apel
abstract: Software engineering students often struggle to appreciate empirical methods and hypothesis-driven inquiry, especially when taught in theoretical terms. This experience report explores whether grounding empirical learning in hype-driven technologies can make these concepts more accessible and engaging. We conducted a one-semester seminar framed around the currently popular topic of AI coding assistants, which attracted unusually high student interest. The course combined hands-on sessions using AI coding assistants with small, student-designed empirical studies. Classroom observations and survey responses suggest that the hype topic sparked curiosity and critical thinking. Students engaged with the AI coding assistants while questioning their limitations -- developing the kind of empirical thinking needed to assess claims about emerging technologies. Key lessons: (1) Hype-driven topics can lower barriers to abstract concepts like empirical research; (2) authentic hands-on development tasks combined with ownership of inquiry foster critical engagement; and (3) a single seminar can effectively teach both technical and research skills.
title_zh: 利用热潮培养实证思维：关于人工智能编程助手的一次教学实践
abstract_zh: 软件工程专业的学生常常难以理解实证方法和基于假设的探究，尤其是在以纯理论方式讲授时。本文通过一个经验报告探讨了：将实证学习建立在当前热门的技术话题之上，是否能让这些概念变得更加易于理解且更具吸引力。我们开展了一门为期一学期的研讨课，围绕当下广受关注的AI编程助手这一主题展开，吸引了远超寻常的学生兴趣。课程结合了使用AI编程助手的实际操作环节，以及由学生自主设计的小型实证研究。课堂观察与问卷反馈表明，这一热点话题激发了学生的好奇心和批判性思维。学生们在实际使用AI编程助手的同时，开始质疑其局限性——这种探索过程培养了他们评估新兴技术相关主张所需的实证思维能力。主要启示包括：（1）以热点驱动的主题能够降低抽象概念（如实证研究）的学习门槛；（2）真实、动手的开发任务与学生对研究过程的自主掌控感相结合，能有效促进深度参与和批判性思考；（3）一门短期研讨课即可有效传授技术技能与科研能力。

title: GPT-NL Public Corpus: A Permissively Licensed, Dutch-First Dataset for LLM Pre-training
authors: Jesse van Oort, Frank Brinkkemper, Erik de Graaf, Bram Vanroy, Saskia Lensink
abstract: We present the GPT-NL Public Corpus, the biggest permissively licensed corpus of Dutch language resources. The GPT-NL Public Corpus contains 21 Dutch-only collections totalling 36B preprocessed Dutch tokens not present in any other LLM pretraining corpus. Additionally, the corpus includes roughly 207B English, 232B Code, and 48B German/Danish tokens taken from existing sets which we further curated for compliance. This corpus includes curated data from large existing corpora like Common Corpus and Common Crawl, as well as newly created Dutch-specific collections. Most newly created Dutch collections consist of content collected in collaboration with organisations or synthetically augmented content. All data is collected and evaluated with the aim of facilitating the creation of (commercial) language models that are lawful, useful and non-harmful. All data included in the GPT-NL Public Corpus is sourced from datasets with permissive licensing and is curated and redistributed under a CC-BY license. The full dataset is publicly available on the Hugging Face Hub.
title_zh: GPT-NL 公共语料库：一种宽松许可、以荷兰语为主的大型语言模型预训练数据集
abstract_zh: 我们发布了GPT-NL公共语料库，这是目前最大且采用宽松许可协议的荷兰语资源语料库。GPT-NL公共语料库包含21个纯荷兰语数据集，总计360亿个经过预处理的荷兰语标记（tokens），这些标记均未出现在任何其他大型语言模型（LLM）预训练语料库中。此外，该语料库还包含约2070亿个英文、2320亿个代码以及480亿个德语/丹麦语标记，这些数据源自现有语料集，我们对其进行了进一步筛选以确保合规性。该语料库整合了来自大型现有语料库（如Common Crawl和Common Corpus）的经筛选数据，以及新创建的专为荷兰语设计的数据集。大多数新创建的荷兰语数据集内容是与相关组织合作收集的，或通过合成方式扩充生成的。所有数据的采集与评估均旨在促进合法、实用且无害的语言模型（包括商业用途）的开发。GPT-NL公共语料库中所包含的所有数据均来自具有宽松许可协议的数据集，并以CC-BY许可协议进行筛选与再分发。完整数据集已公开发布于Hugging Face Hub平台。

title: Executing as You Generate: Hiding Execution Latency in LLM Code Generation
authors: Zhensu Sun, Zhihao Lin, Zhi Chen, Chengran Yang, Mingyi Zhou, Li Li, David Lo
abstract: Current LLM-based coding agents follow a serial execution paradigm: the model first generates the complete code, then invokes an interpreter to execute it. This sequential workflow leaves the executor idle during generation and the generator idle during execution, resulting in unnecessary end-to-end latency. We observe that, unlike human developers, LLMs produce code tokens sequentially without revision, making it possible to execute code as it is being generated. We formalize this parallel execution paradigm, modeling it as a three-stage pipeline of generation, detection, and execution, and derive closed-form latency bounds that characterize its speedup potential and operating regimes. We then present Eager, a concrete implementation featuring AST-based chunking, dynamic batching with gated execution, and early error interruption. We evaluate Eager across four benchmarks, seven LLMs, and three execution environments. Results show that Eager reduces the non-overlapped execution latency by up to 99.9% and the end-to-end latency by up to 55% across seven LLMs and four benchmarks.
title_zh: 执行即生成：在大语言模型代码生成中隐藏执行延迟
abstract_zh: 当前基于大语言模型（LLM）的编程代理遵循串行执行范式：模型首先生成完整的代码，随后调用解释器来执行。这种顺序工作流导致在代码生成期间执行器处于空闲状态，而在执行期间生成器也处于空闲状态，从而造成不必要的端到端延迟。我们观察到，与人类开发者不同，LLMs 会逐个生成代码标记且不进行修改，因此可以在代码生成过程中即开始执行。我们形式化了这一并行执行范式，将其建模为生成、检测和执行三个阶段的流水线，并推导出闭式延迟边界，以刻画其加速潜力和运行区间。随后，我们提出了 Eager——一种具体的实现方案，包含基于抽象语法树（AST）的代码分块、带门控机制的动态批处理以及早期错误中断功能。我们在四个基准测试、七种 LLM 模型和三种执行环境中对 Eager 进行了评估。结果表明，Eager 在七种 LLM 和四个基准上，将非重叠执行延迟降低了高达 99.9%，端到端延迟降低了最高达 55%。

title: LDMDroid: Leveraging LLMs for Detecting Data Manipulation Errors in Android Apps
authors: Xiangyang Xiao, Huaxun Huang, Rongxin Wu
abstract: Android apps rely heavily on Data Manipulation Functionalities (DMFs) for handling app-specific data through CRUDS operations, making their correctness vital for reliability. However, detecting Data Manipulation Errors (DMEs) is challenging due to their dependence on specific UI interaction sequences and manifestation as logic bugs. Existing automated UI testing tools face two primary challenges: insufficient UI path coverage for adequate DMF triggering and reliance on manually written test scripts. To address these issues, we propose an automated approach using Large Language Models (LLMs) for DME detection. We developed LDMDroid, an automated UI testing framework for Android apps. LDMDroid enhances DMF triggering success by guiding LLMs through a state-aware process for generating UI event sequences. It also uses visual features to identify changes in data states, improving DME verification accuracy. We evaluated LDMDroid on 24 real-world Android apps, demonstrating improved DMF triggering success rates compared to baselines. LDMDroid discovered 17 unique bugs, with 14 confirmed by developers and 11 fixed. The tool is publicly available at https://github.com/runnnnnner200/LDMDroid.
title_zh: LDMDroid：利用大语言模型检测Android应用中的数据操纵错误
abstract_zh:
Android 应用高度依赖数据操作功能（Data Manipulation Functionalities, DMFs）来处理应用特定的数据，通过 CRUDS 操作（创建、读取、更新、删除、查询）实现。因此，DMFs 的正确性对于保障应用的可靠性至关重要。然而，检测数据操作错误（Data Manipulation Errors, DMEs）极具挑战性，因为这些错误往往依赖于特定的用户界面（UI）交互序列，并表现为逻辑缺陷。现有的自动化 UI 测试工具面临两大难题：一是难以覆盖足够的 UI 路径以充分触发 DMFs；二是严重依赖人工编写的测试脚本。

为解决上述问题，我们提出一种基于大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）的自动化方法，用于 DME 检测。我们开发了 LDMDroid——一个面向 Android 应用的自动化 UI 测试框架。LDMDroid 通过一种状态感知的流程引导 LLM 生成 UI 事件序列，显著提升了 DMF 触发的成功率。同时，该框架利用视觉特征识别数据状态的变化，从而提高 DME 验证的准确性。

我们在 24 个真实世界中的 Android 应用上对 LDMDroid 进行了评估，结果表明其在 DMF 触发成功率方面优于现有基线方法。LDMDroid 共发现 17 个独特的漏洞，其中 14 个经开发者确认，11 个已被修复。该工具已公开发布，访问地址为：https://github.com/runnnnnner200/LDMDroid。

title: Shapley-Guided Neural Repair Approach via Derivative-Free Optimization
authors: Xinyu Sun, Wanwei Liu, Haoang Chi, Tingyu Chen, Xiaoguang Mao, Shangwen Wang, Lei Bu, Jingyi Wang, Yang Tan, Zhenyi Qi
abstract: DNNs are susceptible to defects like backdoors, adversarial attacks, and unfairness, undermining their reliability. Existing approaches mainly involve retraining, optimization, constraint-solving, or search algorithms. However, most methods rely on gradient calculations, restricting applicability to specific activation functions (e.g., ReLU), or use search algorithms with uninterpretable localization and repair. Furthermore, they often lack generalizability across multiple properties. We propose SHARPEN, integrating interpretable fault localization with a derivative-free optimization strategy. First, SHARPEN introduces a Deep SHAP-based localization strategy quantifying each layer's and neuron's marginal contribution to erroneous outputs. Specifically, a hierarchical coarse-to-fine approach reranks layers by aggregated impact, then locates faulty neurons/filters by analyzing activation divergences between property-violating and benign states. Subsequently, SHARPEN incorporates CMA-ES to repair identified neurons. CMA-ES leverages a covariance matrix to capture variable dependencies, enabling gradient-free search and coordinated adjustments across coupled neurons. By combining interpretable localization with evolutionary optimization, SHARPEN enables derivative-free repair across architectures, being less sensitive to gradient anomalies and hyperparameters. We demonstrate SHARPEN's effectiveness on three repair tasks. Balancing property repair and accuracy preservation, it outperforms baselines in backdoor removal (+10.56%), adversarial mitigation (+5.78%), and unfairness repair (+11.82%). Notably, SHARPEN handles diverse tasks, and its modular design is plug-and-play with different derivative-free optimizers, highlighting its flexibility.
title_zh: 基于导数无优化的Shapley引导神经修复方法
abstract_zh:
深度神经网络（DNNs）容易受到后门、对抗攻击和不公平性等缺陷的影响，从而削弱其可靠性。现有的修复方法主要依赖于重新训练、优化、约束求解或搜索算法。然而，大多数方法依赖梯度计算，限制了其在特定激活函数（如ReLU）上的适用性；而采用搜索算法的方法则往往存在定位与修复过程不可解释的问题。此外，这些方法通常难以在多种属性之间实现通用化修复。

我们提出了SHARPEN，一种将可解释的故障定位与无导数优化策略相结合的新方法。首先，SHARPEN引入了一种基于Deep SHAP的定位机制，用于量化每一层及每个神经元对错误输出的边际贡献。具体而言，该方法采用分层的粗粒度到细粒度策略：先根据各层的综合影响进行重排序，再通过分析违反属性状态与正常状态之间的激活差异，精确定位出故障神经元或滤波器。随后，SHARPEN采用CMA-ES（协方差矩阵自适应进化策略）对识别出的故障神经元进行修复。CMA-ES利用协方差矩阵捕捉变量间的依赖关系，实现无梯度搜索，并能协调调整相互关联的神经元。

通过将可解释的故障定位与进化优化相结合，SHARPEN实现了跨架构的无导数修复，对梯度异常和超参数变化具有更强的鲁棒性。我们在三个修复任务上验证了SHARPEN的有效性：在兼顾属性修复与精度保持的前提下，其在后门清除方面表现优于基线方法10.56%，在对抗攻击缓解上提升5.78%，在不公平性修复上提升11.82%。值得注意的是，SHARPEN能够处理多样化的修复任务，且其模块化设计支持与不同无导数优化器无缝集成，展现出极强的灵活性。

title: Online Reasoning Calibration: Test-Time Training Enables Generalizable Conformal LLM Reasoning
authors: Cai Zhou, Zekai Wang, Menghua Wu, Qianyu Julie Zhu, Flora C. Shi, Chenyu Wang, Ashia Wilson, Tommi Jaakkola, Stephen Bates
abstract: While test-time scaling has enabled large language models to solve highly difficult tasks, state-of-the-art results come at exorbitant compute costs. These inefficiencies can be attributed to the miscalibration of post-trained language models, and the lack of calibration in popular sampling techniques. Here, we present Online Reasoning Calibration (ORCA), a framework for calibrating the sampling process that draws upon conformal prediction and test-time training. Specifically, we introduce a meta-learning procedure that updates the calibration module for each input. This allows us to provide valid confidence estimates under distributional shift, e.g. in thought patterns that occur across different stages of reasoning, or in prompt distributions between model development and deployment. ORCA not only provides theoretical guarantees on conformal risks, but also empirically shows higher efficiency and generalization across different reasoning tasks. At risk level $δ=0.1$, ORCA improves Qwen2.5-32B efficiency on in-distribution tasks with savings up to 47.5% with supervised labels and 40.7% with self-consistency labels. Under zero-shot out-of-domain settings, it improves MATH-500 savings from 24.8% of the static calibration baseline to 67.0% while maintaining a low empirical error rate, and the same trend holds across model families and downstream benchmarks. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/wzekai99/ORCA.
title_zh: 在线推理校准：测试时训练实现可泛化的置信度语言模型推理
abstract_zh: 尽管测试时缩放（test-time scaling）已使大型语言模型能够解决极为复杂的任务，但当前最先进的成果伴随着高昂的计算成本。这些效率低下问题可归因于微调后语言模型的校准不当，以及主流采样技术缺乏校准机制。本文提出一种名为在线推理校准（Online Reasoning Calibration, ORCA）的框架，通过结合置信区间预测（conformal prediction）与测试时训练（test-time training），对采样过程进行校准。具体而言，我们引入了一种元学习机制，针对每个输入动态更新校准模块。该方法能够在分布偏移情况下提供有效的置信度估计，例如在推理不同阶段出现的思维模式变化，或在模型开发与部署阶段提示分布的差异。ORCA不仅在理论上保证了置信区间风险的控制，还在实证中展现出更高的效率和跨不同推理任务的泛化能力。在风险水平 $δ=0.1$ 下，ORCA在分布内任务上将 Qwen2.5-32B 的效率提升了最高达 47.5%（使用监督标签）和 40.7%（使用自洽性标签）。在零样本域外设置下，其将 MATH-500 任务的资源节省率从静态校准基线的 24.8% 提升至 67.0%，同时保持较低的实际误差率；这一趋势在不同模型家族和下游基准测试中均得到验证。我们的代码已公开，地址为 https://github.com/wzekai99/ORCA。

title: Performance Analysis of Compiler Code Generation for HPC and AI Kernels on RISC-V
authors: Rama Kishan Malladi, Nitya Hariharan
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: The open-source RISC-V architecture [1] is well suited for systems that run diverse applications such as HPC, AI, IoT and embedded systems. Performance analysis of various kernels on a RISC-V architecture is necessary while making a choice of an implementation. In the absence of hardware, the LLVM-MCA [2] tool can provide useful information on the implementation of the RISC-V architecture and its impact on the performance of HPC and AI kernels. In this paper, we use the LLVM-MCA tool to analyze the performance of various HPC and AI kernels on a RISC-V implementation by Tenstorrent<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">*</sup>. We look at the performance of these workloads on this RISC-V platform, and discuss the various considerations that determine runtime performance.
title_zh: RISC-V 上高性能计算与人工智能核函数的编译器代码生成性能分析
abstract_zh: 开源的RISC-V架构[1]非常适合运行多种应用，如高性能计算（HPC）、人工智能（AI）、物联网（IoT）和嵌入式系统。在选择实现方案时，对RISC-V架构上各类内核的性能分析是必不可少的。由于缺乏实际硬件，LLVM-MCA[2]工具可以提供有关RISC-V架构实现及其对HPC和AI内核性能影响的有用信息。本文利用LLVM-MCA工具，分析Tenstorrent<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">*</sup>实现的RISC-V平台上各类HPC和AI内核的性能表现。我们考察了这些工作负载在该RISC-V平台上的性能，并讨论了决定运行时性能的各种因素。

title: GPU Kernel-Level Characterization and Optimization of Transformer Models: From Baseline to LoRA Fine-Tuning
authors: Sasi Snigdha Yadavalli, Badrinath Ramamurthy
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: This study addresses the problem of systematically applying hardware-software optimizations to standard transformer training pipelines. The investigation examines how hardware-aware GPU techniques and LoRA hyperparameters jointly influence computational efficiency and model quality. By profiling CUDA kernel-level metrics and model-level scores (BLEU, chrF), we demonstrate how optimizations like FlashAttention and batched memory operations reduce execution time, while LoRA hyperparameters affect both model quality and computational load.
title_zh: GPU内核级Transformer模型表征与优化：从基础模型到LoRA微调
abstract_zh: 本研究探讨了将硬件-软件优化系统性地应用于标准Transformer训练流程的问题。研究分析了硬件感知的GPU技术与LoRA超参数如何共同影响计算效率和模型质量。通过分析CUDA内核级别的性能指标以及模型级别的评分（BLEU、chrF），我们证明了诸如FlashAttention和批处理内存操作等优化技术能够有效减少执行时间，而LoRA超参数则同时影响模型质量和计算负载。

title: An LLM-Assisted Fuzz Testing Framework for Unmanned System Software
authors: Yupeng Zhang, Qing Wei, Shaoxun Liu, Yankun Xu, Haoyang Chen, Jinfeng Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The booming application of unmanned system has led the software vulnerabilities of these systems becoming increasingly prominent. Fuzz testing is an effective approach to detecting software vulnerabilities particularly during the software development phase. However, the requirement for manually written fuzz drivers renders it a resource-intensive and expert-dependent task, which hampers the widespread application of fuzzing. To address these issues, we proposed an automated fuzz testing framework based on large language models (LLMs). First, we employ an intelligent code parsing module to circumvent the context length limitations of LLMs, enabling efficient processing of target project code. Second, we engineer a structured prompt template to guide LLMs in generating high-quality fuzz driver code. Subsequently, to mitigate the inherent propensity of LLMs to produce flawed code, we integrate a feedback-based correction mechanism. Finally, we have deployed an automated fuzzing platform and applied it to several typical software components for unmanned systems. The system performs notably well on relatively simple fuzz testing targets and alleviates the manual workload required for fuzz testing complex targets. Ablation experiments confirm that the feedback-based correction mechanism effectively enhances the performance of LLMs in fuzz driver generation. This framework provides a viable approach to automated fuzz testing and is expected to contribute to vulnerability analysis in unmanned systems.
title_zh: 一种基于大语言模型的无人系统软件模糊测试框架
abstract_zh: 无人系统应用的迅猛发展，使得其软件漏洞问题日益突出。模糊测试（Fuzz Testing）是一种在软件开发阶段有效检测软件漏洞的方法。然而，传统模糊测试需要手动编写模糊驱动程序（fuzz driver），这一过程资源消耗大且高度依赖专家经验，严重制约了模糊测试的广泛应用。为解决上述问题，我们提出了一种基于大型语言模型（LLMs）的自动化模糊测试框架。首先，我们引入智能代码解析模块，突破大型语言模型在上下文长度上的限制，实现对目标项目代码的高效处理；其次，设计结构化提示模板，引导LLMs生成高质量的模糊驱动代码；随后，为缓解LLMs生成有缺陷代码的固有倾向，我们集成了一种基于反馈的纠错机制；最后，我们构建并部署了一个自动化模糊测试平台，并将其应用于多个典型无人系统软件组件中。实验结果表明，该系统在相对简单的模糊测试目标上表现优异，显著降低了复杂目标模糊测试所需的大量人工工作量。消融实验进一步验证了基于反馈的纠错机制能有效提升LLMs在模糊驱动生成方面的性能。本框架为自动化模糊测试提供了一条可行路径，有望在无人系统的漏洞分析中发挥重要作用。

title: hls4ml: A Flexible, Open-Source Platform for Deep Learning Acceleration on Reconfigurable Hardware
authors: Jan-Frederik Schulte, Benjamin Ramhorst, Chang Sun, Jovan Mitrevski, Nicolò Ghielmetti, Enrico Lupi, Dimitrios Danopoulos, Vladimir Loncar, Javier Duarte, David Burnette, Lauri Laatu, Stylianos Tzelepis, Konstantinos Axiotis, Quentin Berthet, Haoyan Wang, Paul White, Suleyman Demirsoy, Marco Colombo, Thea Klaeboe Aarrestad, Sioni Summers, Maurizio Pierini, Giuseppe Di Guglielmo, Jennifer Ngadiuba, Javier Campos, Ben Hawks, Abhijith Gandrakota, Farah Fahim, Nhan Tran, George A. Constantinides, Zhiqiang Que, Wayne Luk, Alexander Tapper, Duc Hoang, Noah Paladino, Philip Harris, Bo-Cheng Lai, Manuel Valentin, Ryan Forelli, Seda Ogrenci, Lino Gerlach, Rian Brooks Flynn, Mia Liu, Daniel Diaz, Elham E Khoda, Melissa Quinnan, Russell Marroquin Solares, Santosh Parajuli, Mark S. Neubauer, Christian Herwig, Ho Fung Tsoi, Dylan Rankin, Shih-Chieh Hsu, Scott Hauck

title: Accelerating Triton convolutions by exposing Shared Memory to the Programmer
authors: Shourya Goel, Ricky Dev, Pradeep Ramachandran
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: With each new generation of NVIDIA GPUs, advancements in memory bandwidth, tensor core throughput, and specialized hardware features necessitate significant redevelopment of CUDA kernels to maximize deep learning model performance. This process is resource-intensive and often results in brittle, hardware-specific code that is challenging to maintain. Triton, a Python-like domain-specific language (DSL), addresses these challenges by offering a block-based programming API that abstracts the complexities of CUDA kernel development.
title_zh: 通过向程序员暴露共享内存来加速Triton卷积运算
abstract_zh: 随着每一代NVIDIA GPU的推出，内存带宽、张量核心吞吐量以及专用硬件功能的提升，都要求对CUDA内核进行大规模重开发，以充分发挥深度学习模型的性能。这一过程耗时耗力，且常常导致代码脆弱、高度依赖特定硬件，难以维护。Triton作为一种类似Python的领域特定语言（DSL），通过提供基于块的编程API，抽象了CUDA内核开发的复杂性，有效解决了这些问题。

title: FPGA Implementation of a Fault-Tolerant Low-Latency Reversible ALU Using Hybrid Parity-Preserving IG–NFT Logic Gates in Verilog HDL
authors: Kattubadi Abdul Raseed, S. Rambabu, Manubolu Venugopal Naidu, C. Gangaiah Yadav, Bikkili Alekya Himabindu
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper presents a fault-tolerant, low-latency reversible Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) designed using hybrid parity-preserving IG and NFT gates in Verilog HDL. Reversible computing minimizes energy dissipation and enables quantumcompatible designs, while parity preservation ensures on-chip error detection. The proposed ALU performs addition, subtraction, AND, and XOR operations with reduced quantum cost, fewer garbage outputs, and lower logic depth compared to conventional and earlier reversible ALUs, such as the hybrid Fredkin–Feynman ALU, Peres–HNG ALU. Synthesized on an Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA board with the VIVADO simulation tool, the design exhibits notable improvements in power efficiency, logic utilisation, and fault tolerance. Scalability for multi-bit operations and compatibility with quantum frameworks make the architecture suitable for low-power embedded systems, IoT processors, and cryptographic applications.
title_zh: 基于Verilog HDL的混合奇偶保持IG-NFT逻辑门实现容错低延迟可逆ALU的FPGA设计
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种基于混合奇偶性保持IG门与NFT门的容错、低延迟可逆算术逻辑单元（ALU），采用Verilog HDL实现。可逆计算能够最小化能量损耗，并支持量子兼容设计，而奇偶性保持机制则实现了片上错误检测。所提出的ALU在执行加法、减法、AND和XOR运算时，相较于传统及早期的可逆ALU（如混合Fredkin–Feynman ALU、Peres–HNG ALU），具有更低的量子代价、更少的垃圾输出以及更低的逻辑深度。该设计在Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA开发板上使用VIVADO仿真工具进行综合，表现出显著提升的能效、逻辑资源利用率以及容错能力。其具备多比特操作的可扩展性，且与量子计算框架兼容，因此适用于低功耗嵌入式系统、物联网处理器以及密码学应用。

title: Leveraging Key Source Code Metrics for Predicting Software Vulnerabilities with Machine Learning
authors: Behzad Khalaji Emamzadeh Abbasi, Alireza Shameli-Sendi
abstract: Security vulnerabilities are a common concern in software systems, posing significant risks such as unauthorized access, data breaches, and system failures, which makes their early detection essential for both organizations and end-users. To address this challenge, various techniques—particularly machine learning—have been explored. This study investigates the key source code factors that contribute to software vulnerability prediction using multiple machine learning models, with the dual objective of detecting vulnerability presence and classifying vulnerability types such as Denial of Service and Buffer Overflow. Unlike prior studies that largely overlook the relationship between code metrics and specific vulnerability categories, this work systematically analyzes these connections to provide actionable security insights. The proposed approach is evaluated on datasets from ten real-world projects, including OpenSSL, SQLite, FreeType, LibTIFF, Libxslt, Binutils, FFmpeg, ImageMagick, OpenSC, and rdesktop, while addressing class imbalance through the SmoteTomek technique. Experimental results demonstrate that Random Forest achieves superior performance, reaching an accuracy of 0.96, precision of 0.948, recall of 0.974, and F1-score of 0.961 in vulnerability detection, along with strong effectiveness in vulnerability type classification. Overall, the findings highlight the most influential source code metrics in vulnerability prediction and confirm the practical value of machine learning for enhancing secure software development.
title_zh: 利用关键源代码度量指标通过机器学习预测软件漏洞
abstract_zh: 安全漏洞是软件系统中常见的关注点，可能带来未经授权的访问、数据泄露和系统故障等重大风险，因此早期检测对组织和最终用户都至关重要。为应对这一挑战，研究人员已探索多种技术，尤其是机器学习方法。本研究通过使用多种机器学习模型，深入分析影响软件漏洞预测的关键源代码因素，旨在实现漏洞存在性的检测以及漏洞类型（如拒绝服务攻击和缓冲区溢出）的分类。与以往研究大多忽视代码度量与特定漏洞类别之间关系不同，本文系统地分析了这些关联，以提供可操作的安全洞察。所提出的方案在十个真实世界项目的数据集上进行了评估，包括OpenSSL、SQLite、FreeType、LibTIFF、Libxslt、Binutils、FFmpeg、ImageMagick、OpenSC和rdesktop，并采用SmoteTomek技术解决类别不平衡问题。实验结果表明，随机森林模型表现优异，在漏洞检测方面达到了0.96的准确率、0.948的精确率、0.974的召回率和0.961的F1分数，同时在漏洞类型分类方面也表现出色。总体而言，研究结果揭示了在漏洞预测中最具影响力的源代码度量指标，并证实了机器学习在提升安全软件开发方面的实际价值。

title: Toward Unified Standards for Open RTL-to-GDSII Design Flows: Insights From the Open RTL-to-GDSII Flow Standards Workshop (ORFSW 2025) [Standards Corner]
authors: Hossam O. Ahmed, Ahmed Madian, Boon Chong Ang, Yongfu Li
abstract: The rapid expansion of open-source Electronic Design Automation (EDA) has created unprecedented opportunities for accessible, transparent, and collaborative chip design. However, the growing diversity of tools, data formats, and methodologies has also highlighted the urgent need for unified standards that ensure interoperability, scalability, and reproducibility across the complete RTL-to-GDSII design flow. The Open RTL-to-GDSII Flow Standards Workshop (ORFSW 2025) brings together academic researchers, industry leaders, open-source developers, and standards experts to explore emerging challenges and opportunities in establishing a cohesive framework for open silicon development. This workshop provides a platform for discussing standardization gaps in RTL design, synthesis, floorplanning, placement, and routing, timing closure, physical verification, and sign-off. Through invited talks, panel discussions, and community-driven dialogue, ORFSW 2025 aims to accelerate the creation of practical, consensus-based standards that enable reliable design portability, enhance toolchain interoperability, and support the long-term sustainability of the open hardware ecosystem. By fostering cross-domain collaboration, the workshop seeks to strengthen the global movement toward democratized chip design and further advance the IEEE CASS Standards Activities Subdivision’s mission to shape the future of open-source EDA and semiconductor innovation.
title_zh: 面向统一的开放RTL到GDSII设计流程标准：来自2025年开放RTL到GDSII流程标准研讨会（ORFSW 2025）的洞见 [标准专栏]
abstract_zh: 开源电子设计自动化（EDA）的迅猛发展，为可访问、透明且协作式的芯片设计创造了前所未有的机遇。然而，工具、数据格式和方法学日益多样化，也凸显出建立统一标准的紧迫需求，以确保从RTL到GDSII整个设计流程中的互操作性、可扩展性和可复现性。2025年开放式RTL至GDSII设计流程标准研讨会（ORFSW 2025）汇聚了学术研究人员、产业领袖、开源开发者及标准专家，共同探讨在构建开放硅芯片开发一体化框架过程中面临的新挑战与新机遇。本次研讨会为讨论RTL设计、综合、布局规划、放置、布线、时序收敛、物理验证及签核等环节中的标准化空白提供了平台。通过主题演讲、圆桌论坛以及社区主导的对话，ORFSW 2025旨在加速制定切实可行、共识驱动的标准，以实现可靠的设计可移植性，提升工具链的互操作性，并支持开源硬件生态系统的长期可持续发展。通过促进跨领域协作，研讨会致力于推动全球范围内芯片设计民主化的进程，进一步推进IEEE CASS标准活动细分组“塑造开源EDA与半导体创新未来”的使命。

title: Sat-Based Model for Verifying Access Policies with Graph Zone Decomposition in Critical Information Infrastructure Networks
authors: Damir R. Musin, Alexandr V. Dushkin
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Industrial firewall rule sets in ICS/CII networks often contain thousands of entries with prefix overlaps, nested groups, negations, and vendor-specific service abstractions. These configurations must be validated offline due to air-gapped architectures and regulatory requirements (IEC 62443, NIST SP 800-82), yet existing verification tools depend on network topology, controller state, or simplified rule semantics. This paper introduces a topology-independent verification framework based on atomic predicate normalization and a communication-induced partition of the address space. We formally prove the correctness of the resulting atomic partition, the completeness of the equivalence-class decomposition, and the SAT-preserving properties of predicate slicing. Experiments on nine mixed real and synthetic ICS datasets (340–6150 rules) show a speedup of 2.4 to 3.1 times over direct SAT, full agreement with SMT/BDD baselines, and stable scaling for industrial rule structures, making the method suitable for offline auditing of critical infrastructure firewalls.
title_zh: 基于卫星的模型在关键信息基础设施网络中利用图区域分解验证访问策略
abstract_zh: 工业控制系统（ICS）/关键信息基础设施（CII）网络中的防火墙规则集通常包含数千条规则，存在前缀重叠、嵌套分组、否定操作以及厂商特定的服务抽象等复杂特征。由于网络采用隔离（air-gapped）架构并受制于监管要求（如IEC 62443、NIST SP 800-82），这些配置必须进行离线验证。然而，现有的验证工具往往依赖于网络拓扑、控制器状态或简化的规则语义，难以满足实际需求。本文提出一种与拓扑无关的验证框架，基于原子谓词归一化和由通信行为驱动的地址空间划分方法。我们形式化证明了所得原子划分的正确性、等价类分解的完备性，以及谓词切片的SAT保持性质。在九个混合真实与合成的ICS数据集（规则数量为340至6150条）上的实验表明，该方法相比直接使用SAT求解器，性能提升2.4至3.1倍；与SMT/BDD基线方法结果完全一致，并在工业级规则结构下表现出稳定的可扩展性，因此非常适合用于关键基础设施防火墙的离线审计。

title: Towards an Agentic AI Design across Edge and Cloud
authors: Shiva Sai Krishna Anand Tokal, Vaibhav Jha, Anand Eswaran, Praveen Jayachandran, Yogesh Simmhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: AI agents typically rely on massive LLMs which are associated with high resource demands and inference costs. This limits the deployment of complex agentic systems on edge devices with limited computational power. Furthermore, existing state-of-the-art agentic workflow patterns often struggle with long context management leading to excessive token usage and increased cost which becomes a problem particularly for smaller models with limited context windows. We define a novel agentic workflow pattern specifically designed for context window efficiency. It decomposes the task into stages to create opportunities for context optimization and efficient information retention across multiple steps of the application. This approach aims to significantly minimizes redundant context, enabling the execution of larger, multi-step agentic applications without exceeding the token limits of Small Language Models (SLMs).
title_zh: 面向边缘与云端的代理型人工智能设计
abstract_zh: AI代理通常依赖于大规模语言模型（LLMs），而这些模型伴随着高昂的资源需求和推理成本，限制了复杂代理系统在计算能力有限的边缘设备上的部署。此外，现有的最先进代理工作流模式在处理长上下文时往往表现不佳，导致令牌使用量过大，进而增加成本，这对上下文窗口较小的模型而言尤为突出。为此，我们提出了一种专为上下文窗口效率优化而设计的新颖代理工作流模式。该模式将任务分解为多个阶段，从而创造机会实现上下文优化，并在应用的多步骤过程中高效保留信息。这一方法旨在显著减少冗余上下文，使更大、多步骤的代理应用能够在不超出小型语言模型（SLMs）令牌限制的前提下顺利执行。

title: PARISCV: A RISC-V Profiler for Application-Specific Hardware Optimization
authors: Spoorthi S, M. R. Ashuthosh, Bharadhwaj K, Vinay Reddy, Madhura Purnaprajna
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: The growing need for faster and more reliable computing has increased the use of application-specific architectures. RISC-V, with its simplicity and modularity, is an ideal platform for such design exploration. Application performance can be improved by adding ISA extensions, but while hardware implementations reduce execution cycles, they also increase area. Finding the right balance between software cycles and hardware cost is therefore crucial. PARISCV is a profiling tool that analyzes RISC-V execution traces to identify beneficial ISA extensions and potential custom instructions. It streamlines performance tuning by highlighting opportunities for acceleration. PARISCV’s extension recommendations achieved up to a 1.4× speed-up on the CV32E40X core with an area overhead of 1.15× and power overhead of 1.01×, while applications that did not require the B extension saw no improvement.
title_zh: PARISCV：面向特定应用硬件优化的RISC-V性能分析工具
abstract_zh: 日益增长的对更快、更可靠计算的需求，推动了专用架构的广泛应用。RISC-V凭借其简洁性和模块化特性，成为此类设计探索的理想平台。通过添加指令集架构（ISA）扩展，可以提升应用性能，但硬件实现虽然减少了执行周期，也带来了面积开销的增加。因此，在软件执行周期与硬件成本之间找到恰当的平衡至关重要。PARISCV是一款分析RISC-V执行轨迹的性能剖析工具，能够识别出有益的ISA扩展及潜在的自定义指令。它通过突出显示加速机会，简化了性能调优过程。在CV32E40X核心上，PARISCV的扩展建议实现了最高1.4倍的性能提升，同时面积开销仅为1.15倍，功耗开销仅1.01倍；而对于不需要B扩展的应用，则未见性能改善。

title: Hardware-Software Co-Design of Post-Quantum FALCON Digital Signature Scheme with RISC-V
authors: Rishabh Shrivastava, Utsav Banerjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: FALCON is a lattice-based digital signature scheme recently selected as NIST post-quantum cryptography standard. FALCON is well known for its fast verification support along with small key and signature sizes. However, implementation of the FALCON signature algorithm in resource-constrained edge computing systems is challenging due to the requirement of complex hashing, floating point arithmetic and polynomial transformation computations. In this work, we demonstrate resource-efficient hardware acceleration of FALCON signature generation and verification by performing hardware-software co-design of its computationally expensive functions with a light-weight 32-bit Vex RISC-V processor core with accompanying custom instructions. We propose Custom Function Units compatible with Vex RISC-V to accelerate Keccak permutations and polynomial transformations, along with a constant-time implementation of IEEE 754 floating-point arithmetic. Our design achieves ≈ 4.3× speedup for signature verification and ≈ 1.6× speedup for signature generation compared to the baseline software on Vex RISC-V for both parameter sets of FALCON, when implemented on AMD Xilinx UltraScale+ FPGA. We also present an efficient verification-only variant of our design suitable for resource-constrained applications.
title_zh: 基于RISC-V的后量子FALCON数字签名方案的软硬件协同设计
abstract_zh: FALCON 是一种基于格的数字签名方案，最近被选为 NIST 后量子密码学标准。FALCON 以其快速的验证支持以及较小的密钥和签名尺寸而著称。然而，由于其在资源受限的边缘计算系统中实现时需要复杂的哈希运算、浮点数运算以及多项式变换计算，因此实现 FALCON 签名算法面临挑战。本文中，我们通过针对计算密集型函数进行软硬件协同设计，并结合轻量级 32 位 Vex RISC-V 处理器核心及其自定义指令，实现了 FALCON 签名生成与验证的高效硬件加速。我们提出了与 Vex RISC-V 兼容的自定义功能单元，用于加速 Keccak 置换和多项式变换运算，同时实现了符合 IEEE 754 标准的恒定时间浮点数运算。在 AMD Xilinx UltraScale+ FPGA 上实现后，我们的设计相较于基准软件在 Vex RISC-V 上对 FALCON 的两种参数集均实现了约 4.3 倍的签名验证加速和约 1.6 倍的签名生成加速。此外，我们还提出了一种适用于资源受限场景的高效仅验证型设计方案。

title: Self-Learning Rule Engine for Automated Compliance Checking of Technical Documents Against Evolving Standards
authors: Ziye Yang, Xinqian Xia, Mengwei Wang, Weiguo Shi, Chaojie Zhu, Chan Shan
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 6th International Conference on Computer Science and Management Technology

title_zh: 面向技术文档合规性自动检查的自学习规则引擎及其对不断演进标准的适应机制

title: AGHE: Generating Enhanced Node Embeddings in Heterogeneous Graphs - Design and Semantic Embedding Compositions
authors: Silvio Fernando Angonese, Renata Galante
abstract: Heterogeneous graphs provide a powerful framework for modeling complex relationships across multiple modalities, such as text, images, and structured subgraphs. A central question addressed in this paper is how to systematically construct heterogeneous graphs enriched with multimodal node representations and compose them into unified embeddings that effectively support node classification tasks. This paper proposes AGHE (Approach for Generating Enhanced Heterogeneous Node Embeddings), a unified and modular pipeline for generating and composing heterogeneous embeddings. AGHE integrates local node features, aggregated neighborhood information, and metapath-based relational semantics into enriched vector representations, enabling more expressive and informative models for downstream classification. Experimental evaluation is conducted across three complementary settings: (i) a controlled multimodal heterogeneous graph, (ii) the IMDB benchmark for comparison with representative heterogeneous GNN architectures, and (iii) a structurally distinct DBLP graph under controlled relation ablation to assess structural sensitivity. Results demonstrate that embedding compositions consistently outperform single embeddings, achieving competitive or superior performance relative to state-of-the-art methods while exhibiting stable behavior across runs. Moreover, structural ablation analysis reveals that composition-based embeddings remain comparatively robust under progressive heterogeneity reduction, mitigating performance degradation when relational diversity is constrained. These findings confirm the effectiveness of modular embedding construction and highlight the importance of composition strategies for advancing robust representation learning in heterogeneous graphs.
title_zh: AGHE：在异构图中生成增强节点嵌入——设计与语义嵌入组合
abstract_zh: 异构图提供了一个强大的框架，用于建模跨多种模态（如文本、图像和结构化子图）的复杂关系。本文解决的一个核心问题是：如何系统地构建富含多模态节点表示的异构图，并将这些表示组合成统一的嵌入表示，以有效支持节点分类任务。为此，本文提出AGHE（生成增强型异构节点嵌入的方法），这是一种统一且模块化的管道，用于生成和组合异构嵌入。AGHE将局部节点特征、聚合的邻域信息以及基于元路径的关系语义整合到丰富的向量表示中，从而为下游分类任务构建更具表现力和信息量的模型。实验评估在三个互补设置下进行：(i) 一个受控的多模态异构图；(ii) IMDB基准数据集，用于与代表性异构图神经网络架构进行比较；(iii) 在受控关系消融条件下，针对结构上不同的DBLP图，评估其对结构敏感性的表现。实验结果表明，嵌入组合方法始终优于单一嵌入方法，在性能上达到或超过当前最先进的方法，且在多次运行中表现出稳定的性能。此外，结构消融分析显示，基于组合的嵌入在逐步降低异质性的情况下仍保持相对鲁棒性，当关系多样性受到限制时，能有效缓解性能下降。这些发现证实了模块化嵌入构建的有效性，并突显了组合策略在推进异构图中稳健表示学习方面的重要性。

title: PEEM: Prompt Engineering Evaluation Metrics for Interpretable Joint Evaluation of Prompts and Responses in LLMs
authors: Minki Hong, Eunsoo Lee, Sohyun Park, Jihie Kim
abstract: Prompt design is a primary control interface for large language models (LLMs), yet standard evaluations largely reduce performance to answer correctness, obscuring why a prompt succeeds or fails and providing little actionable guidance. We propose PEEM (Prompt Engineering Evaluation Metrics), a unified framework for joint and interpretable evaluation of both prompts and responses. PEEM defines a structured rubric with 9 axes: 3 prompt criteria (clarity/structure, linguistic quality, fairness) and 6 response criteria (accuracy, coherence, relevance, objectivity, clarity, conciseness)—and uses an LLM-based evaluator to output (i) scalar scores on a 1–5 Likert scale and (ii) criterion-specific natural-language rationales grounded in the rubric. Across 7 benchmarks and 5 task models, PEEM’s accuracy axis strongly aligns with conventional accuracy while preserving model rankings (aggregate Spearman ρ ≈ 0.97, Pearson r ≈ 0.94, p < 0.001). A multi-evaluator study with four models shows consistent relative judgments (pairwise ρ = 0.68–0.85), supporting evaluator-agnostic deployment. Beyond alignment, PEEM captures complementary linguistic failure modes and remains informative under prompt perturbations: prompt-quality trends track downstream accuracy under iterative rewrites, semantic adversarial manipulations induce clear score degradation, and meaning-preserving paraphrases yield high stability (robustness rate ≈ 76.7–80.6%). Finally, using only PEEM scores and rationales as feedback, a zero-shot prompt rewriting loop improves downstream accuracy by up to 11.7 points, outperforming supervised and RL-based prompt-optimization baselines. Overall, PEEM provides a reproducible, criterion-driven protocol that links prompt formulation to response behavior and enables systematic diagnosis and optimization of LLM interactions.
title_zh: PEEM：用于可解释性联合评估提示与响应的提示工程评估指标（适用于大语言模型）
abstract_zh:
提示设计是大型语言模型（LLM）的主要控制接口，然而现有的标准评估方法大多仅将性能简化为答案的正确性，忽略了提示成功或失败的深层原因，难以提供可操作的改进指导。为此，我们提出了PEEM（提示工程评估指标），一个统一的框架，用于对提示和响应进行**联合**且**可解释**的评估。PEEM定义了一个包含9个维度的结构化评分标准：3个提示维度（清晰度/结构、语言质量、公平性）和6个响应维度（准确性、连贯性、相关性、客观性、清晰度、简洁性）。该框架采用基于大语言模型的评估器，输出（i）1–5分制的标量评分，以及（ii）基于评分标准的、针对各维度的自然语言推理说明。

在7个基准测试和5种任务模型上的实验表明，PEEM的“准确性”维度与传统准确率高度一致，同时保持了模型间的排名顺序（总体Spearman相关系数ρ ≈ 0.97，Pearson相关系数r ≈ 0.94，p < 0.001）。通过四个模型的多评估者研究发现，相对判断结果具有高度一致性（成对相关系数ρ = 0.68–0.85），支持评估器无关的部署应用。除了良好的对齐性外，PEEM还能捕捉到互补的语言学失败模式，并在提示扰动下依然保持信息有效性：提示质量的趋势与迭代重写后的下游准确率变化一致；语义对抗性修改导致评分明显下降；而语义保持的改写版本则表现出高稳定性（鲁棒性率≈76.7%–80.6%）。

最后，仅使用PEEM的评分和理由作为反馈，一个零样本提示重写循环可使下游准确率最高提升11.7个百分点，优于监督学习和强化学习基线的提示优化方法。总体而言，PEEM提供了一套可复现、以准则为导向的评估协议，将提示设计与响应行为紧密关联，实现了对LLM交互过程的系统性诊断与优化。

title: MATANALYZE - A Custom Accelerator Generator for Sparse × Dense and Dense × Sparse Matrix Multiplication
authors: Mohammed Sahel, Disha Kiran D, M. R. Ashuthosh, Madhura Purnaprajna
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Matrix multiplication is fundamental to deep learning, scientific computation, and graph analytics. The prevalence of sparse matrices in these fields offers significant opportunities to reduce memory and compute costs. optimizing compression formats and algorithms is crucial for improving execution time, memory footprint, and energy efficiency.This paper introduces MATANALYZE, a framework designed to generate efficient hardware accelerators for sparse × dense and dense × sparse matrix multiplication. MATANALYZE takes into account the hardware constraints, including the number of DSPs and BRAMs and the characteristics of the input matrices (sparsity and dimensions) and generates optimized custom hardware accelerator designs. The hardware designs explored by MATANALYZE are based on either row-wise or column-wise product algorithms. Compared to conventional methods, these approaches offer more favorable trade-offs between performance and resource utilization. This paper demonstrates that, for a given workload, selecting the appropriate combination of compression format and multiplication algorithm can lead to significant performance improvements, achieving in some cases more than 1000× speedup compared to naive baseline implementations.
title_zh: MATANALYZE - 用于稀疏×稠密和稠密×稀疏矩阵乘法的自定义加速器生成器
abstract_zh: 矩阵乘法是深度学习、科学计算和图分析领域的基础。这些领域中稀疏矩阵的广泛应用，为降低内存和计算成本提供了巨大机遇。优化压缩格式与算法对于提升执行时间、减少内存占用以及提高能效至关重要。本文提出了一种名为MATANALYZE的框架，旨在为稀疏×稠密矩阵乘法及稠密×稀疏矩阵乘法生成高效的硬件加速器。MATANALYZE综合考虑了硬件约束（如DSP和BRAM的数量）以及输入矩阵的特性（稀疏度和维度），并生成经过优化的定制化硬件加速器设计方案。MATANALYZE所探索的硬件设计基于行优先或列优先的乘法算法，相较于传统方法，这些方案在性能与资源利用率之间提供了更优的权衡。本文表明，针对特定工作负载，选择合适的压缩格式与乘法算法组合，可带来显著的性能提升，在某些情况下甚至比原始基线实现快1000倍以上。

title: Digital Twins Reimagined: Zero-Day LLM-Powered Moving Target Defense In-Depth for Real-Time CPS
authors: Rajarshi Mukherjee, Mohamed Azab, Thidapat Chantem
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) revolution has profoundly transformed modern industries by embedding connected digital devices across critical sectors such as energy, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing. Such interconnected devices together form Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS), complex structures that enable computation to directly control physical processes. These systems increasingly rely on automation and real-time control to enhance efficiency, productivity, and safety. However, this growing dependence on connectivity has significantly expanded the attack surface, exposing mission-critical infrastructure to sophisticated cyber threats. Among the most dangerous are zero-day vulnerabilities, which are unknown flaws in software or firmware, and zero-day design flaws, which are deeply embedded architectural weaknesses often undetectable by conventional verification methods. Traditional defense mechanisms, largely based on static rules or known threat signatures, are ill-equipped to detect these stealthy and evolving threats - especially in real-time systems, where even minor delays in detection or response can result in catastrophic outcomes. To overcome these limitations, this work presents a transformative security framework that leverages the reasoning and generative capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to construct an intelligent, redundant digital twin of the operational system. Unlike traditional replicas, the LLM-driven digital twin is logically and architecturally isolated, making it inaccessible to attackers targeting the primary system. Crucially, the twin is not a static copy: it is dynamically synthesized and continuously restructured by the LLM to ensure a heterogeneous and evolving codebase. This ensures functional alignment with the physical system while introducing deliberate software diversity, thereby increasing attacker uncertainty and resilience to shared exploits. The central hypothesis of the framework is that semantic deviations between the decisions or behaviors of the digital twin and its physical counterpart can serve as reliable indicators of anomalous activity—whether stemming from external attacks or latent design flaws. By monitoring this divergence, the system facilitates the early detection of zero-day threats and supports real-time mitigation strategies, thereby significantly strengthening the resilience of critical infrastructure. Implementation results demonstrated the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed approach.
title_zh: 数字孪生重构：基于零日大语言模型的动态目标防御深度解析，适用于实时网络物理系统
abstract_zh:
物联网（IoT）革命已深刻重塑现代产业，通过在能源、制药和制造等关键领域广泛部署互联数字设备，推动了技术的全面升级。这些相互连接的设备共同构成了网络物理系统（CPS），即能够实现计算与物理过程直接交互的复杂体系结构。随着自动化和实时控制在这些系统中日益普及，其效率、生产力与安全性均得到显著提升。然而，这种对连通性的高度依赖也大幅扩展了攻击面，使关键基础设施面临日益复杂的网络威胁。其中最具破坏性的威胁之一是“零日漏洞”——指软件或固件中尚未被发现的缺陷；另一类则是“零日设计缺陷”，即深植于系统架构中的隐蔽弱点，往往无法通过传统验证手段察觉。

传统的防御机制大多依赖静态规则或已知威胁特征，难以应对这类隐蔽且不断演化的威胁，尤其在实时系统中，哪怕微小的检测或响应延迟也可能导致灾难性后果。为突破上述局限，本文提出一种变革性的安全框架，利用大型语言模型（LLM）的推理与生成能力，构建一个智能、冗余的运行系统数字孪生体。与传统静态复制不同，该LLM驱动的数字孪生体在逻辑与架构上与主系统完全隔离，因而无法被针对主系统的攻击者访问。更为关键的是，该孪生体并非一成不变：它由LLM动态生成并持续重构，确保代码基底具有异构性和演化性。这不仅保证了与物理系统的功能一致性，还引入了刻意的软件多样性，从而增加攻击者的不确定性，并有效抵御共性漏洞的利用。

该框架的核心假设是：数字孪生体与其物理实体在决策或行为上的语义偏差，可作为异常活动的可靠指示信号——无论其源于外部攻击还是潜在的设计缺陷。通过持续监测这种差异，系统能够实现对零日威胁的早期发现，并支持实时缓解策略，显著增强关键基础设施的韧性。实验结果表明，所提方法在有效性与效率方面均表现出色。

title: Large Language Models for Malware Detection: A Systematic Review, Taxonomy, and Open Challenges
authors: Joyce Champie, Karim Elish
abstract: The rapid evolution of malware targeting mobile devices, IoT/edge platforms, and traditional computing systems has intensified the need for detection techniques capable of reasoning about complex code semantics and behavioral patterns. Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently emerged as a promising direction due to their ability to encode contextual relationships that are difficult to model with traditional approaches. This topical review presents a comprehensive synthesis of LLM-driven malware detection across heterogeneous environments. Based on 26 peer-reviewed studies published between 2020 and 2025, the review introduces a unified taxonomy spanning static and behavioral embeddings, prompt-driven analyses, reasoning-enhanced architectures, and security-oriented fine-tuning strategies. A structured comparative assessment across effectiveness, robustness, computational efficiency, explainability, and privacy, reveals consistent methodological trends: fine-tuned and hybrid transformer architectures deliver the strongest empirical performance, while prompt-based and lightweight models offer practical benefits for resource-constrained platforms. The review also highlights persistent gaps, including limited adversarial-robustness evaluation, insufficient attention to privacy and governance, and a lack of standardized metrics for explainability and energy efficiency. The study concludes by outlining key research challenges and future opportunities, including scalable robustness testing, privacy-preserving LLM adaptation, lightweight architectures for constrained devices, and more rigorous human-centered explainability benchmarks. This review aims to consolidate existing knowledge and guide the development of reliable and practical LLM-driven malware detection systems.
title_zh: 大型语言模型在恶意软件检测中的应用：一项系统性综述、分类体系与开放性挑战
abstract_zh: 针对移动设备、物联网/边缘平台及传统计算系统日益演化的恶意软件，其复杂代码语义与行为模式的检测需求愈发迫切。近年来，大型语言模型（LLMs）因其能够捕捉传统方法难以建模的上下文关联关系，展现出巨大的应用潜力。本文综述系统性地整合了跨异构环境的LLM驱动恶意软件检测研究进展。基于2020至2025年间发表的26篇同行评审文献，本综述提出一个统一的分类体系，涵盖静态与行为嵌入、提示驱动分析、增强推理架构以及面向安全的微调策略。通过在有效性、鲁棒性、计算效率、可解释性及隐私保护等方面的结构化对比评估，揭示出若干一致的方法论趋势：经过微调的混合Transformer架构在实证性能上表现最优，而基于提示和轻量级模型则在资源受限平台中具备实际优势。同时，本文也指出了当前存在的持续性短板，包括对抗鲁棒性评估不足、对隐私与治理关注不够，以及缺乏可解释性与能效的标准度量指标。最后，研究展望了关键的研究挑战与未来机遇，如可扩展的鲁棒性测试、隐私保护的LLM适配、面向受限设备的轻量化架构，以及更严格的以用户为中心的可解释性基准。本综述旨在整合现有知识，推动可靠且实用的LLM驱动恶意软件检测系统的研发。

title: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Automated Code Changes: A Systematic Review
authors: Padamjit Sandhu, Pankaj Khurana, Jimmy Singla
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been employed more and more to automate code changes (ACC) for a range of development projects, such as enhancements in features, refining and error remediation. Recently, a number of LLM based automate code change paradigms have been created and reviewed. Although LLMs in ACC tasks have been studied in past research, this domain still needs to evaluate uniform procedures, a standardized dataset and reducibility challenges. Therefore, this study provides a systematic analysis of existing literature on LLM based ACC tasks to examine the core data sets, frequently employed assessment metrics and persistent reducibility issues. Findings of this review study revealed that the most frequently used dataset to train an LLM model for code change is Defects4J. Similarly, repair success rate, pass@k and patch accuracy were the main evaluation measured utilized in the articles to evaluate the performance of the developed model. Moreover, this study also examined various reproducibility challenges that can be faced when recreating a similar ACC model. These results open the door to more investigation into how to fully utilized LLMs to transform automated code changes.
title_zh: 大型语言模型在自动化代码修改中的基准测试：一项系统性综述
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在越来越多的开发项目中被用于自动化代码修改（ACC），涵盖功能增强、代码优化及错误修复等多种任务。近年来，已涌现出多种基于LLM的自动化代码修改范式，并得到了系统性研究与评估。尽管以往研究已对LLM在ACC任务中的应用进行了探索，但该领域仍亟需统一的评估流程、标准化的数据集以及可复现性挑战的深入分析。因此，本研究对现有基于LLM的ACC相关文献进行了系统性综述，重点考察了核心数据集、常用评估指标以及持续存在的可复现性问题。研究发现，Defects4J是训练LLM模型进行代码修改时最常使用的数据集；而修复成功率（repair success rate）、pass@k和补丁准确率（patch accuracy）则是论文中普遍采用的主要评估指标。此外，本研究还深入探讨了在复现类似ACC模型过程中可能遇到的各种可复现性挑战。这些发现为未来更充分地利用LLM推动自动化代码修改技术的发展提供了重要启示。

title: Agentic AI Software Engineers: Programming with Trust
authors: Abhik Roychoudhury, Corina Păsăreanu, Michael Pradel, Baishakhi Ray

title_zh: 代理型AI软件工程师：以信任为基础的编程
