title: AutoTuneGPU: An Energy-Aware Autotuning Framework for Machine Learning Workloads on GPUs
authors: Ananya Krishna Srivastava, Kumari Soumya, Gargi Alavani Prabhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Energy efficiency is an increasing priority for machine learning (ML) developers deploying large-scale workloads on GPUs. However, selecting optimal launch configurations such as grid and block dimensions, threads per block, shared memory allocation, and register usage, remains a manual, performance-centric process that often neglects power consumption. We present AutoTuneGPU, a data-driven autotuning framework that automatically recommends energy-efficient kernel configurations tailored to a given thread count. Our approach first builds a configuration–performance–power dataset by profiling representative CUDA benchmarks that mirror core ML primitives, including matrix multiplication, convolution, reduction, scan, and transpose. We then apply Bayesian Optimization and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) on this dataset to explore the design space efficiently and identify configurations that minimize power usage without sacrificing performance. Once trained, AutoTuneGPU can, given only the number of threads, predict an optimal combination of launch parameters and resource allocations.
title_zh: AutoTuneGPU：一种面向GPU上机器学习工作负载的节能自调优框架
abstract_zh: 能效正日益成为机器学习（ML）开发者在GPU上部署大规模工作负载时的优先考虑因素。然而，选择最优的启动配置（如网格和块维度、每块线程数、共享内存分配以及寄存器使用等）仍然是一种手动且以性能为中心的过程，常常忽视了功耗问题。我们提出了AutoTuneGPU，这是一个数据驱动的自动调优框架，能够根据给定的线程数量，自动推荐节能的内核配置。我们的方法首先通过分析代表性的CUDA基准测试来构建一个包含配置—性能—功耗的数据集，这些基准测试模拟了核心机器学习原语，包括矩阵乘法、卷积、归约、扫描和转置操作。随后，我们在该数据集上应用贝叶斯优化与粒子群优化（PSO），高效探索设计空间，识别出在不牺牲性能的前提下最小化功耗的配置方案。训练完成后，AutoTuneGPU仅需输入线程数量，即可预测出最优的启动参数组合与资源分配方案。

title: Efficient Widening Operations for Static Analysis of Bit-Vector Programs
authors: Guangsheng Fan, Dengping Wei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Static analysis of bit-vector programs is crucial for detecting bugs such as integer overflow, yet it remains challenging due to their reliance on machine integer semantics and the intricate interplay between arithmetic and bitwise operations. To tackle this difficulty, the recently proposed swb abstract domain combines bit-level and word-level abstractions. Its bit-level component generalizes the tnum abstraction—originally used in the Linux eBPF verifier—into a signedness-aware, standard abstract domain. However, the current widening operator employed in this bit-level abstraction is overly conservative, often resulting in substantial analysis overhead.In this paper, we propose a significantly more efficient widening operator for tnum and the bit-level abstraction of swb, inspired by the characteristics of bit-vector operations. Unlike existing widening operations, our design can maintain stable bits while accelerating the iteration of other higher bits to achieve convergence. Experiments on the SV-COMP benchmarks show that the generalized tnum abstraction equipped with our new widening operator achieves a speedup of up to 23.51× in analysis time over the original approach. When integrated into the full swb domain, it still accelerates analysis by up to 1.93×. Crucially, in both settings, the optimized abstract domain validates all programs that the original domain could verify, demonstrating that our optimization incurs no loss in practice verification capability.
title_zh: 位向量程序静态分析中的高效扩展操作
abstract_zh: 位向量程序的静态分析对于检测整数溢出等缺陷至关重要，但由于其依赖机器级整数语义以及算术运算与位运算之间复杂的相互作用，该任务仍然极具挑战性。为应对这一难题，近期提出的swb抽象域结合了位级和字级抽象。其位级组件将原本用于Linux eBPF验证器的tnum抽象推广为一种考虑符号性、标准形式的抽象域。然而，当前该位级抽象所采用的widening操作过于保守，常常导致显著的分析开销。本文提出了一种更为高效的tnum及swb位级抽象的widening操作，其设计灵感来源于位向量运算的特性。与现有方法不同，我们的设计能够在保持稳定位不变的同时，加速其他高位的迭代过程，从而实现更快的收敛。在SV-COMP基准测试上的实验表明，采用新widening操作的广义tnum抽象相比原方法，分析时间最高可提升23.51倍；当集成到完整的swb域中时，分析速度仍可提升最高达1.93倍。关键的是，在两种场景下，优化后的抽象域均能验证原域所能验证的所有程序，证明了本优化在实际验证能力上无任何损失。
